data.title: Alibaba beats Q3 estimates
data.description:
• Alibaba press release (NYSE:BABA): Q3 Non-GAAP EPADS of $2.93 beats by $0.27.
  • Revenue of $38.38B (+24.9% Y/Y) beats by $130M.
  • For the quarter ended December 31, 2024, revenue from Cloud Intelligence Group was RMB31,742 million (US$4,349 million), an increase of 13% year-over-year.
  • Income from operations was RMB41,205 million (US$5,645 million), an increase of 83% year-over-year, primarily due to the decrease in impairment of intangible assets as well as the increase in adjusted EBITA.
  • Diluted earnings per ADS was RMB20.39 (US$2.79). Diluted earnings per share was RMB2.55 (US$0.35 or HK$2.75). Non-GAAP diluted earnings per ADS was RMB21.39 (US$2.93), an increase of 13% year-over-year. Non-GAAP diluted earnings per share was RMB2.67 (US$0.37 or HK$2.88), an increase of 13% year-over-year.

data.title: Boxlight prices offering to raise ~$2.8M
data.description:
• Boxlight (NASDAQ:BOXL) is offering 1,323,000 shares of its Class A common stock and warrants to purchase up to 1,323,000 shares in a private placement priced at-the-market under Nasdaq rules.
  • The combined purchase price of each share (or common stock equivalent in lieu thereof) together with the accompanying warrant is $2.13.
  • The warrants will have an exercise price of $2.13 a share, will be exercisable six months following the date of issuance and will expire five and a half years from the date of issuance.
  • The gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be around $2.8M. The company intends to use the net proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: NICE gives mixed Q4 results
data.description:
• NICE press release (NASDAQ:NICE): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.54 misses by $0.46.
  • Revenue of $721.6M (+15.8% Y/Y) beats by $6.74M.
  • Total annual revenue growth of 15% year over year to $2.7 billion
  • Record high annual operating margin drives record EPS and 48% year over year growth in cash flow from operations to $833 million

data.title: VGP NV reports FY results
data.description:
• VGP NV press release (OTCPK:VGPBF): FY net profit of € 287 million, an increase of € 200 million or 229% versus FY’23.
  • Net asset value growth of 8.4%, up to € 2.4 billion.
  • EBITDA growth of 57% with solid contribution from recurring rental business(1) activities of € 204.3 million (+19%), from development activities in amount of € 144.8 million (+178%) and in renewable energy of € 5.4 million (+236%).
  • Liquidity position of € 493 million (vs € 210 million dec ’23), € 500 m undrawn credit facilities, a gearing ratio of 33.6% (vs 40.3% dec ’23) and a proportional LTV(6) of 48.3% (versus 53.4% dec’ 23). EPRA NTA is up 7%.

data.title: Birkenstock Holding reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Birkenstock Holding press release (NYSE:BIRK): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of €0.18
  • Revenue of €362M (+19.5% Y/Y).
  • Strong double-digit revenue growth across all segments including 16% in the Americas, 17% in EMEA and 47% in APAC in reported and constant currency
  • B2B revenue growth of 30% in reported and constant currency
  • DTC revenue growth of 11% on a reported basis and 10% in constant currency
  • Gross profit margin of 60.3%, down 70 basis points from 61.0% in the first quarter of 2024 primarily due to the increase in B2B share relative to a year ago
  • Net profit of EUR 20 million, up from a Net loss of EUR 7 million; EPS of EUR 0.11, up from EUR (0.04)
  • Adjusted Net profit of EUR 33 million, up 99% from EUR 17 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of EUR 102 million, up 25% year-over-year; Adjusted EBITDA margin of 28.2%, up 130 basis points from 26.9% a year ago

data.title: Bilibili reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Bilibili press release (NASDAQ:BILI): Q4 Adjusted net profit1 was RMB452.0 million (US$61.9 million) for the fourth quarter, compared with an adjusted net loss of RMB555.8 million in the same period last year. For 2024, adjusted net loss was RMB39.0 million (US$5.3 million), narrowing by 99% year over year.
  • Revenue of $1.06B (+18.5% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • Average daily active users (DAUs) were 103.0 million in the fourth quarter, compared with 100.1 million in the same period last year.

data.title: Grieg Seafood reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Grieg Seafood ASA press release (OTCPK:GRGSF): Q4 GAAP EPS of -NOK14.20.
  • Revenue of NOK 2.25B (+16.0% Y/Y).
  • Operational EBIT in the quarter of NOK -74 million (NOK -67 million), with operational EBIT/kg of NOK -3.1 (-3.1).
  • Finnmark impacted by string jellyfish attack at Vinnalandet, triggering a cost increase of NOK 75 million. Good production at the other sea sites
  • Stable and good growth in Rogaland and Newfoundland, improved biological production in British Columbia
  • Target harvest volume of 84 000 tonnes for the full year 2025 ◦ Expect to harvest 18 800 tonnes in Q1 2025

data.title: Schneider Electric S.E. Non-GAAP EPS of €8.32, revenue of €38.15B; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Schneider Electric S.E. press release (OTCPK:SBGSF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of €8.32.
  • Revenue of €38.15B (+6.3% Y/Y).
  • FY24 Adj. EBITA €7.1 billion, up +14% organic o Adj. EBITA Margin 18.6%, up +90bps organic.
  • Free Cash Flow €4.2 billion; Cash Conversion1 at 99%.
  • Progressive dividend2 at €3.90/share, up +11%.
  • FY25 Target: Adj. EBITA org. growth between +10% to +15%, driven by +7% to +10% org. revenue growth and +50bps to +80bps org. Adj. EBITA margin improvement.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: PLTR, CVNA, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged down on Thursday morning as investors digested the minutes from the Federal Reserve’s latest policy meeting, which highlighted concerns that Trump’s policy proposals, particularly tariffs, could reignite inflation in the U.S.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Bausch Health (NYSE:BHC) surged over 8% after reporting better-than-expected Q4 results, with a 6% rise in reported revenue and a 9% increase on an organic basis. The company’s 2025 guidance projects revenue between $9.9B and $10.15B, with a midpoint of $10.03B, exceeding the consensus estimate of $9.91B. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to range from $3.53B to $3.68B, while adjusted cash flow from operations is forecasted between $975M and $1.025B.
  • Herbalife (NYSE:HLF) shares jumped 18% after delivering strong Q4 results and announcing a leadership transition, with President Stephan Gratziani set to become CEO on May 1, 2025, while current CEO Michael Johnson will move to Executive Chairman. The company’s outlook projects Q1 net sales declining 1.5% to 5.5% Y/Y, though constant currency sales could be flat to up 4%, with adjusted EBITDA expected between $140M and $150M. For FY2025, net sales are forecasted to range from a 3% decline to a 3% increase, while constant currency sales could rise 1% to 7%, and adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between $600M and $640M.

Biggest stock losers

  • Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) fell over 5% after a memo from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, obtained by the Washington Post, revealed plans for an 8% annual cut to the defense budget over the next five years. The proposed reductions, due by Feb. 24, raised concerns about potential impacts on defense contracts. However, 17 categories are reportedly exempt, including border security, nuclear weapons modernization, missile defense, and one-way attack drones.
  • Shares of Carvana (NYSE:CVNA) dropped around 10% despite a strong Q4 performance, which saw the company beat top- and bottom-line estimates, increase car sales, and improve profit per vehicle. While Carvana expects “significant growth” in retail units sold and adjusted EBITDA in FY2025, with a sequential increase in both metrics in Q1, investor concerns may have been driven by its Form S-3 filing with the SEC, allowing for the potential sale of an undisclosed number of shares. The company’s long-term targets include gross profit growth of 15%-19% and an EBITDA margin of 8%-13.5%.

data.title: Krones AG reports FY results
data.description:
• Krones AG press release (OTCPK:KRNNF): FY revenue rose by 12.1% year on year to €5,293.6 million.
  • Earnings before taxes increased slightly more than EBITDA in 2024, improving by 22.9% from €310.5 million in the previous year to €381.6 million. 
  • EBITDA went up in 2024 by 17.5% year on year, from €457.3 million to €537.1 million. The EBITDA margin rose from 9.7% to 10.1%. This placed it within the full-year 2024 guidance range of 9.8% to 10.3%.
